items.briefing:
A phishing kit called Kali365, flagged by the FBI, is expanding its reach and being used to steal login credentials through convincing fake login pages. Separately, a campaign called DriveSurge has hijacked thousands of websites to trick visitors into running malicious software through fake browser update prompts — a tactic known as FakeUpdate. Both threats are active globally and could easily affect Australian staff browsing the web or checking email.

Boards and leadership teams should be aware that cyber risk oversight is becoming a growing expectation for organisations. If you visit a website and are unexpectedly prompted to update your browser, close the tab immediately and update your browser only through its official settings.

featured.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
featured.quote: AI systems can be socially engineered to bypass security, exposing major platforms to account takeover attacks.
